item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-14
item.description: The development of 5 core modules has been completed (support for high-definition datasets, tag mixing analysis, ARI stability testing, patch size experimentation, and integration of neighborhood query). 18GB of checkpoint data has been uploaded to the Athena server. The conflict issues in the TokenMonitor code have been resolved. The configuration issues related to gadgets and Rclone have also been addressed.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-13
item.description: All the main tasks in all four projects have been completed: the draft MIHD paper, as well as the multi-modal fusion diagnostic solution; the upgrade of the dual audio transcription function of MeetingHelper; the error recovery benchmark architecture and the writing of the NeurIPS report; the bug fixing of the BOSS LeRobot system; in addition, research on the basic model related to batteries has also been completed.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-12
item.description: Advanced development processes were developed on two projects: MIHD space transcriptomics benchmark planning and error recovery documentation/troubleshooting.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-11
item.description: A highly intensive technical workday across four devices: completed BetterSSH full-stack project from zero to production, optimized multi-project development tools, deeply refactored MimicGen augmentation system, constructed a NeurIPS academic writing framework, expanded speech recognition backend

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-10
item.description: Multi-project development day: Completed the writing of the MIHD benchmark test documentation. Fixed compatibility issues between MeetingHelper and various programs. Debugged LIBERO and battery management processes. Also implemented OpenClaw functionality, with no technical debt issues arising. Additionally, improved the pricing accuracy of TokenMonitor to better meet Claude Code’s requirements.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-09
item.description: Full-stack development day: Optimized robot learning pipelines in tianhe (MimicGen analysis + manual collection workflows + multi-data set comparative learning), resolved infrastructure issues (Node proxy, Claude Code permissions), carried out large-scale gadget refactoring (using Ollama for Chine…

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-08
item.description: Completed cross-platform code optimization and translation automation, debugged HPC/robot projects, migrated translation infrastructure to local Ollama, and reimplemented robot enhancement systems from action replay to Waypoint execution

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-07
item.description: Fixed 4 bugs in the website/update.sh script and set up a complete Hugo deployment environment. The website update process recovered from complete failure to successful completion in all 7 steps. A full daily/weekly MCP synchronization process was also initiated.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-06
item.description: Completed the planning and execution of three annotations in the MIHD paper draft, including revising section P1 of the introduction, expanding the abstract and R5/R6 to 6 paragraphs, and adding a QueST+TLS reference.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W14
item.description: 本周有三条任务同时推进：MIHD多模态空间转录组论文准备工作（5天，DCC负责）已经完成，包括TEDDY全量嵌入基准测试、Leiden对比实验、HD融合评估以及论文双语框架撰写，从而确定了“零样本FM嵌入融合”差异化研究的方向；TokenMonitor Linux FloatBall系统的修复工作也已完成，经过多轮迭代，从症状修复发展到固定窗口+GDK Input Shape架构重构，同时解决了PR #4中全部21个代码审查问题；Life-Copilot Discord Bot的重构工作也完成了，删除了2823行代码，引入了多CLI Agent架构，并实现了屏幕时间追踪、飞书输入渠道、MCP工具集成等多项新功能。此外，还完成了NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track中63篇论文的调研工作，以及新的Linux工作站环境的搭建。

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-05
item.description: By restructuring Linux FloatBall with fixed windows and GDK Input Shape, we effectively resolved platform-level geometric problems.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-04
item.description: Daily AI conversation summary.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-03
item.description: 🔄 Additional runs were conducted for HD fusion experiments, paper narrative design, and document reorganization 15:24:09.206 | claude_code Throughout the day, MIHD paper preparation was progressed at DCC: 4 fusion SLURM jobs based on HD learning were submitted (staig ARI=0.258/llava ARI=0.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-02
item.description: Daily AI Conversation Summaries

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-01
item.description: Daily AI conversation summary.

item.title: Monthly Summary 2026-03
item.description: This month’s work was highly focused on the MIHD spatial transcriptomics multimodal clustering research project: after completing environment initialization on the DCC HPC cluster, we systematically integrated the TEDDY-G 400M foundation model, Leiden clustering algorithm, and STHD pathology annotation Ground Truth. Multi-encoder benchmarks revealed STAIG framework’s high robustness to gene encoders and the systematic transfer loss of Foundation Models on clustering tasks. The paper’s Fig 1–6 framework was finalized, marking the project’s transition from the experimental phase to the paper-writing phase.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-31
item.description: I have completed this week’s tasks: integrating the Leighton clustering algorithm, STHD benchmark data, conducting baseline tests on DLPFC, and evaluating HD multimodality. Currently, we have obtained important experimental data.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-30
item.description: Completed the benchmark tests for TEDDY-G 400M embedding extraction and multi-strategy fusion for all 11 DLPFC slices. W13 weekly report was written, summarizing last week’s research findings.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W13
item.description: This week’s core work focused on multi-model embedding expansion and issue triage for the MIHD spatial transcriptomics project. On March 23, environment initialization was completed on the DCC HPC cluster, and historical challenges in the MIHD project were systematically documented — for the first time structuring the root cause of cross-section retrieval failure (incomparable embedding spaces) and the single-section ARI gap as formal documents. On March 29, four critical bugs in the TEDDY foundation model extraction script were successfully fixed, the first DLPFC section embedding extraction was completed, and batch runs were launched, establishing the foundation for a multi-model comparison benchmark.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-29
item.description: The TEDDY single-cell basic model has been analyzed on the HPC cluster. In the extraction script, four critical issues were resolved, and now the embedding process for the DLPFC dataset is successfully completed.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-28
item.description: The debugging skills for cchypothesis have been improved, with the use of a more intelligent two-track troubleshooting method. Additionally, issues related to jitter in the TokenMonitor dashboard and window positioning have been resolved.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-27
item.description: We have created a debugging technique based on hypothetical assumptions, and utilized this technique to solve the complex issue of the flashing interface of the TokenMonitor. In the solution, we used various placeholder elements such as tokens, URLs, file paths, and code identifiers.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-26
item.description: The capabilities of ccplan were systematically enhanced through 5 major iterations (prompt calibration, multi-intent decomposition, functional protection protocols). Comprehensive refactoring of TokenMonitor was completed, including file splitting, type safety, and cache optimization.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-25
item.description: Solution: Used ccplan skills for structured refactoring: split by responsibility into scout/sub-packages (config/prompts/search/evaluate/report/project/cli), retained thin shims and provided deprecation warnings to maintain backward compatibility.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-24
item.description: ✅ The summarize module has been extensively refactored using ccplan. 20:28:25.974 | claude_code The ccplan skill was used to refactor the 2930-line daily_summary.py into 8 modular components.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-23
item.description: The DCC HPC cluster has been initialized using the rclone synchronization technology. Additionally, relevant configurations have been completed on the GPU nodes. Currently, several issues encountered in the MIHD research project have been identified and documented.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W12
item.description: This week, we worked across three devices: DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop. We conducted comprehensive research in two key areas: robotic operations and spatial transcriptomics. The Error Recovery Benchmark project was completed from design of the collection scheme (329 demo cases for RBG grouping) to architectural-level trajectory segmentation using InteractionSegmenter. In total, 1,627 training scenarios were generated, covering 148 subtypes, representing a 35% increase in data coverage. MIHD spatial transcriptomics further improved by integrating cross-slice embedding techniques, establishing scGPT’s zero-shot advantage with a 100% accuracy rate compared to UNI2’s 71%. For π₀.₅, we completed the full-chain training of the detection head, achieving an error rate of approximately 0.253. We also developed five conditioning strategies for Exp5-9 scenarios. The gadget toolchain was upgraded toopus+max thinking, with improvements in Research Profiler architecture, unified deployment of websites, and full upgrades for ECC devices. This week’s key breakthroughs focused on “identifying and resolving root causes at the architectural level”: issues such as non-comparability between per-section PCA coordinate systems, ambiguity in target_object classification for multiple objects, and compatibility between Flax NNX inheritance and combination techniques were all fundamentally resolved this week.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-22
item.description: ✅ Batch-generated historical weekly reports for W05-W12 19:30:00.000 | claude_code The daily_summary.py tool was used to generate structured weekly reports for weeks 5-12 in 2026.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-21
item.description: The development environment has been configured properly. The critical vulnerabilities in the summary generation process have been fixed. Support for Codex has been implemented, and all AI agents have been upgraded to Opus 4.6 version.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-20
item.description: On the MacBook, generated daily reports for 2026-03-19, fully updated and verified the consistency of all documentation and code repository (7 inconsistencies fixed), identified errors in rclone sync-all (missing reports caused repeated calls of LLM), and executed a complete git history cleanup op…

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-19
item.description: We have conducted extensive research and development across various devices. Space transcriptomics alignment experiments revealed the trade-offs involved in semantic batch mixing. The robot error recovery process, from training optimization to portable deployment, has been validated through 139 tests. CalendarPro/Life Copilot achieves complete architectural restructuring, eliminating bottlenecks associated with single-intent classifiers while supporting the classification of multiple intentions. Additionally, we have expanded our comprehensive knowledge management infrastructure, including improved tools for analyzing long-text data. Yuke Zhu completed a comprehensive analysis of his academic career from 2012 to 2026, publishing over 100 papers and undergoing more than ten iterative analyses. Finally, we performed batch processing on the historical daily reports for the 30 days between February and March 2026.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-18
item.description: In CalendarPro, a batch update feature has been added. This feature allows for the processing of multi-task status reports. This feature ensures comprehensive testing coverage, along with corresponding documentation explanations.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-17
item.description: Cross-device collaboration on MIHD spatio-metrics refactoring, in-depth research on QCVLA’s potential bridging architecture, improvement measures inspired by FLARE, fixes to VLA training/inference infrastructure, generation of error benchmark data, and enhancement of system tools

item.title: Enes Eken — Researcher Profile
item.description: Enes Eken의 학문적 경력은 세 단계로 나뉩니다. 첫 번째 단계는 2014년부터 2018년까지였습니다. 이 기간 동안 그는 스핀 트랜스폼 메모리(STT-RAM)의 신뢰성 설계에 집중했습니다. 특히, 읽기/쓰기 성능 향상, 변동 감지 시뮬레이션 도구, 그리고 오류 수정 코드 관련 기술을 크게 발전시켰습니다. 이 시기에 발표된 논문들이 그의 가장 중요한 연구 성과였습니다.

item.title: Fan Chen — Researcher Profile
item.description: Fan Chen: 在这个数据集中，“Fan Chen”很可能指的是多位同名研究者的合并记录，并非某一位学者的完整研究轨迹。根据论文内容来看，可以明显区分出至少两类截然不同的研究者：（1）一位长期从事分析化学/食品化学研究的研究者，其研究时间贯穿了1992年至2022年，他的研究方向从早期的法医气相色谱技术发展到了……

item.title: Hsin-Pai Cheng — Researcher Profile
item.description: Hsin-Pai Cheng is a researcher specializing in efficient AI systems and edge-based deployments. His academic journey can be divided into three distinct stages. From 2016 to 2018, he focused on the IBM TrueNorth neuromorphic computing platform, conducting deep research into the quantization of neural networks under hardware constraints, as well as aspects related to adversarial robustness. He also made significant contributions to the field of “computational capabilities”.

item.title: Ismail Bayram — Researcher Profile
item.description: Analysis Mode: fast | Analysis Time: 2026-03-16T00:58:48

item.title: Jiachen Mao — Researcher Profile
item.description: Jiachen Mao: Jiachen Mao的学术轨迹极为罕见，表现出多次跨领域的转型。他难以被归类为传统的“领域大师/新星”。在2015年至2018年期间，他的主要研究成果集中在建筑能耗模拟和城市微气候领域。他在高层建筑通风、能耗校准优化以及城市热岛效应分析方面发表了多篇重要论文。

item.title: Jie Guo — Researcher Profile
item.description: Jie Guo: It is necessary to point out a key issue: based on the actual content of the paper list, this does not represent the academic trajectory of a single researcher. Instead, it is the result of incorrect aggregation of data from multiple researchers with the same name (Guo Jie/Guo Jie). The papers with high citations between 2000 and 2005 (with a total of over 2000 citations) originated from a scholar who focused on ** Adolescent Substance Abuse and Social Development**.

item.title: Jun Lu — Researcher Profile
item.description: Jun Lu: Jun Lu’s academic career can be divided into two distinct phases. The first phase (1989–2013) was dedicated to numerical calculations of electromagnetic fields, with a particular focus on the Harmonic Balance Finite Element Method and the design of high-frequency planar transformers. During this period, he established a solid theoretical foundation in the field of nonlinear magnetic field analysis. The key turning point occurred around 2013–2015.

item.title: L. Teng — Researcher Profile
item.description: Analysis Mode: fast | Analysis Time: 2026-03-16T00:17:54

item.title: Linghao Song — Researcher Profile
item.description: Linghao Song is a researcher specializing in computer architecture. His research focuses on memory-based computing as the core theme throughout his work. In 2017, he won the Best Paper Award for his work on ReRAM-based graph processing accelerators. This achievement laid the foundation for his future contributions in this field.

item.title: Nathan Inkawhich — Researcher Profile
item.description: Nathan Inkawhich is a researcher who specializes in tackling the intersection of machine learning and SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) remote sensing recognition technologies. Since 2017, he has been working on both defense/remote sensing applications and deep learning security. His academic career reached its peak during 2019-2020, when he presented papers at CVPR and NeurIPS.

item.title: Wu Lin — Researcher Profile
item.description: Wu Lin: It should be noted that there is a significant issue of confusion regarding the names of these researchers. “Wu Lin” is a common Chinese name, and the database contains the research achievements of at least five researchers with similar names from different fields: ① Plant Soil Ecologists (regulatory mechanisms/rhizosphere microorganisms, 2001-2025, forming the core focus); ② Computer Vision Researchers (person recognition, 2

item.title: Xiangliu Chen — Researcher Profile
item.description: Xiangliu Chen — Early researcher

item.title: Xiaoxiao Liu — Researcher Profile
item.description: Xiaoxiao Liu: It should be noted that there is a significant name ambiguity problem in this paper list. The papers cover a wide range of unrelated fields, including cancer biology, GPU architecture, plant immunity, hyperspectral image processing, genomics, and LLM prompt engineering. Clearly, these papers were contributed by multiple researchers with the same name.

item.title: Xiongfei Yu — Researcher Profile
item.description: Analysis Mode: fast | Analysis Time: 2026-03-16T00:07:51

item.title: Yan Yang — Researcher Profile
item.description: Yan Yang — Early-stage researcher profile (rating: 25.5/100).

item.title: Yanyan Chen — Researcher Profile
item.description: Yanyan Chen: Warning! The academic profile of “Yanyan Chen” suffers from a serious issue of name ambiguity. It is highly likely that Semantic Scholar has combined several researchers with the same name as “Yanyan Chen” into one profile.

item.title: Yingying Huang — Researcher Profile
item.description: Yingying Huang: 从论文列表中可以看出，“Yingying Huang”很可能是因为同名不同人的数据合并结果，而非某个单一研究者的学术成果。这些论文涉及了化学生物学领域（DNA与小分子相互作用、天然产物）、等离子体物理学（GS重建）、凝聚态物理学（VO₂相变）、表面物理学，以及机器学习等领域。

item.title: Yiran Chen — Researcher Profile
item.description: Yiran Chen: Yiran Chen’s academic journey can be divided into three distinct stages. The first stage (2003-2007) focused on optimizing the power consumption of traditional computer architectures, with deterministic clock gating being a key technique used in VLSI design. This period marked his solid foundation in this field. The second stage (2008-2015) saw him turning to emerging storage technologies as his research focus.

item.title: Yongfeng Ding — Researcher Profile
item.description: Yongfeng Ding is an emerging researcher in the early stages of their academic career. They began publishing papers in 2023, and currently have only four publications to their name. Therefore, they do not yet possess the characteristics of a ’leading expert in the field’. However, they have demonstrated certain levels of originality in the area of quantitative analysis of composite materials’ microstructure. Their key contributions focus on the concept of ‘randomness’, which was introduced by them in 2023.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-16
item.description: The work on the embedding of essential models for space transcriptomics has been completed. A error recovery process was designed and implemented, resulting in a data reduction of 81%. Key flaws in the robosuite coordinate system were also fixed. Large-scale scenario generation tasks have been initiated. In collaboration with over 30 researchers, academic analysis tools have been systematically developed.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W11
item.description: This week, six projects were carried out concurrently on three devices: DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop. The projects include the following key tasks: ① MIHD spatial transcriptomics identified fundamental flaws in the cross-sample embedding methodology (independent processing per section leads to non-comparable feature spaces), and efforts are underway to fix these issues. ② ErrorRecoveryBenchmark improved from bug fixes to a comprehensive scale-up, covering 13 skills/29 subtypes. The project also addressed the issue of objects not falling when dropping skills, and after identifying limitations in online quota architectures, a new approach for offline injection was established. ③ VLA-RoboTwin/pi05 completed the entire process from environment setup, training performance optimization (JAX version alignment, 33% speed improvement) to data collection, and auxiliary task experiments. ④ The gadget toolchain upgraded its architecture to include MCP Server, common/shared packages, and unified output directories. Research profilers enabled researchers to prioritize student discoveries on scholar pages. ⑤ CalendarPro underwent comprehensive optimization in 7 stages, with all 230 tests passing. Token consumption was reduced by 40-60%. ⑥ The gadget research toolchain integrated citation graph analysis and completed profiles of 7 renowned AI scholars.

item.title: Haiyong Wang — Researcher Profile
item.description: Haiyong Wang — Early-stage researcher profile (rating: 29.3/100).

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-15
item.description: Full optimization of CalendarPro has been completed. A 7-stage parallel implementation plan was adopted (passed 230 tests). Various toolkits were integrated, allowing for the unification of paper retrieval, performance analysis, and citation information into a unified command-line interface. Shared tool code was extracted and stored in public folders or packaged to avoid writing over 400 lines of duplicate code. All output directories were organized into a structured hierarchical file system. Additionally, collaboration with 13 leading researchers in robotics/artificial intelligence led to the establishment of a systematic academic research tracking mechanism.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-14
item.description: This is a GPU-based monitoring script that can be utilized in user interfaces, HDF5 data visualization tools, as well as the four-group progress prediction experiments involving the pi05 model. It is important to keep the fields marked with placeholders, such as token, URL, file path, and code identifiers, intact.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-13
item.description: ✅ Research Tool Design and Complete Implementation 20:08:05 | claude_code A complete academic researcher analysis tool was designed and implemented for the robotics field.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-12
item.description: We have resolved the issue with sandbox permissions for the macOS Wallpaper application. Additionally, problems related to PyTorch dependencies and CUDA compilation have been addressed. These improvements ensure the proper functioning of the deep learning evaluation environment.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-11
item.description: Multi-project efficiency improvement: refactoring of MIHD files, optimization of K8s GPU monitoring tools, and optimization of VLA training processes (through improvements in the Torch/TorchVision library. The speed has been increased by 33% using JAX technology, while dependencies have also been optimized).

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-10
item.description: Multi-project development day, including documentation cleanup, spatial transcriptomics debugging, robotics learning optimization, and environment troubleshooting across over 6 codebases

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-09
item.description: Completed the core functions of MIHD spatial transcriptomics: cross-sample RM-IDEAL evaluation, CrossModalEnhancer for cross-modal enhancement, and scGPT literature research. Promoted several VLA/robot projects (UniVLA document processing, RoboTwin data conversion, process matching analysis). Implemented the MCP server-based tool for integration with CalendarPro, enhancing the multi-data-source support capabilities of research_scout.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W10
item.description: This week, we conducted parallel efforts across three devices: DCC, Tianhe Supercomputer, and TzJsDesktop. The main projects include space transcriptomics research, robot operation training/ evaluation, AI personal assistants, and paper management tools. The MIHD project completed the full set of scGPT+UNI2 integration experiments (QFormer avg ARI=0.370, +117% compared to scGPT-only), and established a zero-shot cross-sample evaluation framework. Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning achieved an overall success rate of 58.9%, significantly outperforming BC-RNN (0%). The VLA model was quantitatively validated to demonstrate its advantages. CalendarPro made a significant leap from passive response to active decision-making, achieving perfect results in 321 tests. Additionally, BackgroundCoordinator’s critical integration defects were identified and fixed. Gadget Research Scout went from zero build to production-ready status within a single day, completing two-stage LLM paper evaluation pipelines and providing three research directions for further exploration. Error_recovery_benchmark completed the cleanup of all 65 symlinks and established infrastructure for visualizing error scenarios in MP4 format. The most important engineering lessons learned this week are: successful testing does not mean system availability (integration layers must be verified independently), and it is essential to study the architecture of mature projects before designing Agent-based systems.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-08
item.description: Expanded the research tool, added author search functionality, and updated documentation; generated visual charts for error benchmarking tests; designed a critical region annotation system for VLA training and fixed issues with RoboTwin’s rendering infrastructure.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-07
item.description: Parallel operations were carried out on four devices: development of the MIHD documentation system and related research work for scGPT was completed. The FLARE/Phoenix training tools were also launched. Four evaluation issues related to BC-RNN were resolved. The wallpaper function for desktop video pages was implemented using WKWebView. Historical report data for February, spanning over 20 days, was generated in bulk. Development of the context-based intent classification feature in CalendarPro was also completed. Additionally, a complete scientific paper management system was built from scratch, featuring language configuration, reverse initialization, and duplicate removal capabilities.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-06
item.description: The work across projects covered spatial transcriptomics data analysis, CalendarPro error fixing and comprehensive documentation writing, refactoring the robot learning code library, solving Docker rendering stack issues, and designing the Phoenix/FLARE training process.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-05
item.description: Cross-device parallel development, including the use of MIHD spatial transcriptomics technology (through RM-IDEAL cross-sample comparison analysis, to reveal performance characteristics at various levels); improvements and updates to device toolkits (monthly summaries of updates and comprehensive guides to Claude code); debugging efforts on the VLA/Openpi-moe training process (resolved issues during curobo installation and defects in normalization processes). Additionally, a comprehensive upgrade was carried out to the CalendarPro personal assistant system, with 16 new functions added. A code audit was also conducted to identify bugs and implement mechanisms for handling risks.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-04
item.description: Key Insight: In active projects, document drift is inevitable—a systematic alignment process between code and documentation is needed. Parallel proxy exploration mode works well for large codebases: one proxy per main directory, then integrating findings.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-03
item.description: On the DCC platform, the implementation of the MIHD cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark test was completed (including design, 781 lines of script code, error fixing, visualization processing, and 459 lines of documentation). Additionally, data from 13 days of historical AI conversation logs was processed using a MacBook, resulting in structured daily reports. The BC-RNN/Pi0.5 evaluation process was also built, and some key model configuration issues were identified on the Tianhe platform. Finally, the interface was optimized, and relevant documentation for the website was prepared.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-02
item.description: Fixed critical bugs in MIHD scGPT benchmark test, identified STAIG’s architectural limitations; resolved Desktop Video macOS sandbox permission issues using security-grade bookmarks; monitored large-scale Pi0.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W09
item.description: This week, we have been working concurrently on two projects within the DCC and Tianhe clusters: Space Transcriptomics (MIHD) and the Error Recovery Benchmark. On the DCC side, we fixed a critical weight loading bug in scGPT, resulting in an average improvement of 44.4% for ARI tasks. We also extended the MIHD benchmark to the Visium HD dataset and completed a major refactoring of the code involving approximately 250,000 lines of code. On Tianhe, we established a new BC-RNN Phoenix training pipeline with 9 parallel tasks. We identified and fixed the root cause of the task distribution mismatch that led to zero success rates in the Pi0.5 evaluation. This led to key conclusions from the M14 baseline evaluation: the learning strategy achieved SR≈0% in error scenarios, proving the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. Additionally, we successfully stabilized the parallel fine-tuning of Pi0.5 LoRA on 6×A800 hardware.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-01
item.description: Cross-device work was extremely challenging: we completed the integration of the MIHD high-definition dataset and verified the multi-source annotations. We also carried out extensive refactoring of the large code base (cleaning over 250,000 lines of code). Additionally, we generated 28 historical daily reports and optimized the workflow. Meanwhile, we fixed vulnerabilities in the BC-RNN/Pi0.5 training process, enabling parallel training on 6 GPUs. Finally, version 4.1 of the desktop version was released, featuring improvements to the playback function and a redesigned Liquid Glass interface.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-28
item.description: Daily AI conversation summary.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-27
item.description: In the Tianhe cluster, we conducted a thorough evaluation of the allocation of storage space and the guidelines for using Slurm. The results of the M14 benchmark test showed that the BC-RNN algorithm achieved a success rate of 0.28% (5 successes out of 1800 cases), while both Pi0 and Pi0.5 strategies failed to achieve any success. Interestingly, the random algorithm actually outperformed all other strategies, achieving a success rate of 0.0199. Out of the total of 649 instances where PickPlace errors occurred, failures were observed in every case, and the distribution showed significant deviations.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-26
item.description: On the Tianjin supercomputer, we systematically studied the fundamental differences between the training and evaluation processes of Pi0.5/BC-RNN, and addressed these issues. Our evaluation framework has been extended to include 5 MimicGen tasks, and parallel training of BC-RNN image patterns has also begun. We also explored the infrastructure requirements for evaluating checkpoints of Pi0.5 between 10,000 and 100,000 steps. The LaTeX resume format was optimized for processing on MacBook computers.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-23
item.description: Daily AI Dialogue Summary

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W08
item.description: This week, we focused on the MIHD spatial transcriptomics project. We completed a systematic investigation into H&E Image-Only clustering (establishing a literature baseline for ARI values ranging from 0.11 to 0.16), implemented three self-supervised enhancement methods (SCAN improved ARI from 0.251 to 0.303, a +20.6% increase), and established a two-stage framework for Vision Refinement. Meanwhile, we continued to work on Error Recovery Benchmarking on the tianhe cluster (M14 evaluation of infrastructure, initialization of full-scenario evaluations with 649 scenarios). We also prepared the Phoenix pi0.5 data pipeline (storage of 18.4GB MimicGen dataset, training configuration ready). Throughout the week, we resolved several engineering issues, including STEGO NaN, dual normalization bugs, version conflicts with lerobot, and problems with HuggingFace proxies. The remaining issues related to Pi0.5 OOM and visualizing scenes via video verification will be addressed next week.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-22
item.description: Daily AI conversation summary.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-21
item.description: Daily AI Dialogue Summary:

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-20
item.description: Daily AI conversation summary.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-19
item.description: Solution: The only clear report on PFM using only image data was found in the SpConTDS paper. The results of stMMC ablation were cited (ARI improvement rate was 4.6%), to quantify the contribution of images, and HEST-1k was used to provide relative rankings for PFM.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-18
item.description: This work was carried out in parallel on two HPC servers: DCC and Tiansheng. The MIHD multimodal fusion benchmark test with corrected coordinates was monitored, and UNI/UNI2 literature standards were studied.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-17
item.description: Daily AI Dialogue Summary:

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-16
item.description: The comprehensive VLA integration process has been completed for the error recovery benchmark tests (versions 4.3 to 4.5). Successful diagnosis and analysis of the crashes in the MIHD clustering structure have been carried out. Additionally, a feature for generating dialogue summaries for daily device reports has been implemented.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W07
item.description: This week, we focused on three areas: robot simulation, bioinformatics, and toolchain development. In the error_recovery_benchmark project, deep blocking issues were encountered during force injection debugging (30N applied to the OSC controller had no noticeable effect), revealing fundamental problems with the controller’s compensation mechanisms. The MIHD project successfully completed the Chinese documentation for its enhancement plan. ccusage achieved support for GLM multi-model billing through type/form validation checks. Robobrain_pi confirmed that the training process is now ready for use. This week, AI revealed obvious pattern-matching defects in handling repeated errors (SSH lacking the cd command) and environmental assumptions (proxy, pnpm). Human intervention provided more effective debugging strategies during critical decision-making moments, such as extreme force testing, local GPU detection, and reusing existing pricing documents.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-15
item.description: Difference Analysis: Human direct observation is more reliable than AI analysis of indirect indicators. AI mistakenly believed it could rely on indirect indicators instead of direct evidence, indicating that human perception has limitations when unable to view videos.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-14
item.description: Daily AI conversation summary.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-13
item.description: Daily AI Conversation Summaries

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-12
item.description: The MIHD project planned and initiated six improvement directions: standardization, Q-Former, QueST integration, and configuration refactoring. Work included revising the CLAUDE.md document, documenting the improvement plan, and creating standardized tools.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-10
item.description: A PCA+UNI+STAIG fusion spatial transcriptomics benchmark test was conducted on the DCC cluster. Successfully processed 7 out of 11 slides, and visualization results with metric annotations were generated for all slides.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-09
item.description: In the MIHD project’s clustering analysis and RM-Iideal visualization charts, a quantitative overlay layer has been added. Baseline tests and RM-Iideal evaluations were conducted on all DLPFC slices.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W06
item.description: This week (2026-02-06~07), the work was focused on two main areas: first, systematically diagnosing the ARI performance gap between staig_fusion in the MIHD project and the original STAIG (0.21→target 0.56). Five key implementation differences were identified and quantified.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-08
item.description: Difference Analysis: Users have a clearer understanding of the final project form, providing a complete definition.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-07
item.description: On the DCC HPC cluster, we have addressed three critical issues within the MIHD benchmark framework: errors encountered during SVD operations executed by mclust, performance problems during patch extraction, and alignment issues in STAIG’s strict mode. As a result, ARI’s score improved from 0.09 to 0.5435. Additionally, we have completed the initialization work for the QueST project’s documentation, and studied various aspects related to RM metrics.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-06
item.description: The process for submitting public benchmark tests based on GitHub Pages has been implemented, and it is equipped with an interactive user consent mechanism. All placeholders that need to be retained, such as “token”, “URL”, file paths, and code identifiers, have been properly handled.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-05
item.description: In the DCC cluster, we have addressed four key differences between the STAIG implementation and the original paper. We have fully implemented the end-to-end GCN+STAIG fusion module, and resolved all related vulnerabilities. Additionally, we have provided flexible gene preprocessing support. Through parameter comparisons, we identified the root causes of performance differences between MIHD and the original STAIG.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-03
item.description: After discovering the partially completed experiment_log.md file and the missing PDF report, we have successfully generated a complete visualization and documentation for the MIHD benchmark test. All the placeholders, such as tokens, URLs, file paths, and code identifiers, have been properly handled.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-02
item.description: We have investigated the differences in results obtained from the MIHD benchmark tests. The actual ARI score was approximately 0.45, while the recorded result was only 0.37.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-01
item.description: The performance issues with STAIG ARI have been resolved. Now, the MIHD GCN implementation is consistent with the original STAIG. It has been determined that the root cause of the problem lies in an incorrect setting for the gene feature dimensions.

item.title: LeetCode Daily 2025-12-05
item.description: Solution to 3432. Count Partitions with Even Sum Difference

item.title: Weekly Summary 2025-W40
item.description: This week (2025-W40) only had valid conversation records on October 2nd. The conversation remained in the initialization phase. Users opened the core tracking and segmentation files of the AutoSeg-SAM2 project but did not engage in any substantive development work.

item.title: Bug Journal 2025-10-02
item.description: In the AutoSeg-SAM2 project, the relevant source files were opened, but the session did not progress significantly.

item.title: LLM Study
item.description:
Part ChatGPT 4o

ChatGPT 4o API demo

from openai import OpenAI
model_use = "gpt-4o-2024-08-06"
client = OpenAI(api_key="Your-API-key")
completion = client.beta.chat.completions.parse(
	model=model_use,
	messages=[
		{"role": "system", "content": "Extract the event information."},
		{"role": "user", "content": "Alice and Bob are going to a science fair on Friday."},
	],
)
event = completion.choices[0].message.parsed

Note: I tried to use model “gpt-4o” but failed.

How to create ChatGPT API Key

  1. Log in to openai
  2. Use the search bar to search “API keys”
  3. Create a new secret key (Shown only once, invisible after closing the tab)
  4. Go to billing to add some credit to the account

Part UniTox ChatGPT

Read from fda.gov

  1. Read the label of the drug we are interested in from a .csv file.

item.title: LeetCode Daily 2025-01-01
item.description: 1422. Maximum Score After Splitting a String

item.title: Random Ideas
item.description:
1. Does the RAM of a laptop continue to consume power even when the screen is turned off?
  2. Images inserted using Markdown do not automatically appear on the website.
  3. Add a checkbox to the “Random” function.
  4. English version of “Desktop Video” √
  5. What is the Duoboy algorithm?
  6. Multilingual support for “Desktop Video” √
  7. What makes a product a luxury item?
  8. Playing “Desktop Video” in the lockscreen mode.
  9. M4 Pro vs. M3 Max

Zijia (Thomas) Tang

Email: zijia.tang@duke.edu | Phone: +1 (858) 340-5757

Interest Areas: Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, AI4Science, Bioinformatics

Education

Duke University | Aug 2024 – Present

  • Major: Computer Science (B.S.)
  • GPA: 4.0 / 4.0
  • Relevant Coursework: Applied Machine Learning, Advanced Algorithms, Linear Algebra

Selected Publications

FLARE: A Failure-Aware Framework for Autonomous Correction and Recovery in VLA Co-First Author | CVPR 2026

  • Addressed the “brittleness” of current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models where minor perturbations lead to catastrophic failure, aiming to endow robots with human-like resilience.
  • Proposed a novel Retry & Reset paradigm and designed a Perturbation & Bridging augmentation pipeline that systematically decouples robot pose from environment state to resolve In-Distribution (ID) errors.
  • Integrated Gemini-2.5-Pro as an offline failure analyst to mine object-centric “Reset” skills for Out-of-Distribution (OOD) recovery. Implemented using LoRA fine-tuning on Pi_0.5 backbones.
  • Achieved an 84.0% success rate across 9 contact-rich tasks in RoboMimic, outperforming previous SOTA by 26.2%.

scDrugMap: Benchmarking Large Foundation Models for Drug Response Prediction Co-Author | Published in Nature Communications (Impact Factor: 15.7)

item.title: Benchmark
item.description: <iframe src="] " style="width:100%;height:80vh;border:none;" loading="lazy"></iframe>
